summary: Target's stock shows signs of life, finally.

headline: Target quietly expands controversial items, winning back customers
summary: In October 2025, Target made one particularly interesting move to win back a number of customers, and succeeded. Now, it's quietly expanding that program that yielded results. Additionally, in 2025 the retail giant worked hard to earn back consumers’ trust lost over the last couple of years ...

raw.headline: Target quietly expands controversial items, winning back customers

headline: Target Announces 2026 Bullseye Builds, Investing in Community Spaces with Volunteerism and Design
summary: Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT) today announced the timing and locations for its 2026 Bullseye Builds with Community Program — a signature community activation from the retailer that brings together Target's volunteerism and design expertise to meet local needs. Target will complete 13 Bullseye Builds in 2026 in neighborhoods where the company operates, supported by a $1 million investment, hands-on volunteer efforts of Target team members and the partnership of nonprofits and community members t

headline: Walmart Expands and Modernizes Stores to Support Omnichannel Growth
summary: Walmart plans more than 650 U.S. remodels and about 20 new stores in 2026 and early 2027, adding pickup, delivery and digital tools for omnichannel growth.

raw.headline: Walmart Expands and Modernizes Stores to Support Omnichannel Growth
raw.summary: Walmart plans more than 650 U.S. remodels and about 20 new stores in 2026 and early 2027, adding pickup, delivery and digital tools for omnichannel growth.

headline: Can Dollar General Sustain Margin Expansion in Fiscal 2026?
summary: Dollar General's FY25 margin surge from lower shrink may cool in FY26 as remodel and IT spending, tariffs, fuel costs and inflation test gains.

headline: Guggenheim Maintains Buy on Target, Raises Price Target to $140
summary: Guggenheim  analyst John Heinbockel   maintains Target (NYSE:TGT) with a Buy and raises the price target from $130 to $140.
